title: Active customer (definition)
source: Data Governance Council / Customer Analytics Standard v3
content: An ACTIVE CUSTOMER is a customer who has placed at least one order with status 'completed' in the trailing 90 days. Customers with only 'cancelled' or 'returned' orders in that window are NOT active. In the demo schema this maps to customers joined to orders WHERE orders.status = 'completed' AND orders.order_date >= NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'. Do not count customers by signup date alone.

title: Net revenue (definition)
content: NET REVENUE = gross order amount minus refunds and discounts, excluding tax. In the demo schema, use SUM(orders.amount - orders.discount - orders.refund) over orders WHERE status = 'completed'. Never include cancelled or returned orders in net revenue. Tax (orders.tax) is excluded from net revenue.

title: High-value customer (definition)
content: A HIGH-VALUE CUSTOMER is an active customer whose lifetime net revenue is at least 1000 (USD). Compute lifetime net revenue per customer as the sum of completed-order net revenue, then filter to >= 1000.

title: Churned customer (definition)
content: A CHURNED CUSTOMER is a customer who was active in the past but has NOT placed a completed order in the last 180 days. Identify customers whose most recent completed order_date is older than NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days'.

title: Downtown / city-center reference point
content: The canonical DOWNTOWN (city center) reference point for store-proximity analysis is longitude -122.3321, latitude 47.6062 (Seattle, WA), in SRID 4326 (WGS84). When a user asks for things 'near downtown' or 'within N km of downtown', use this point: ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(-122.3321, 47.6062), 4326). Distances are measured in metres after casting to geography.

title: PostGIS spatial conventions
content: All geometry in the demo database uses SRID 4326 (WGS84 longitude/latitude). Store locations are in the stores.geom column, type geometry(Point,4326). To compute distances in METRES, cast geometry to geography: stores.geom::geography. For 'within X km' queries use ST_DWithin(stores.geom::geography, <point>::geography, X*1000). Use ST_Distance for exact distances (metres) and ST_AsGeoJSON(stores.geom) when results should be plotted on a map.

title: Store geometry column
content: The stores table has a geom column of type geometry(Point,4326) holding each store's longitude/latitude. stores.city and stores.region are descriptive only; always use geom for distance/containment, not text matching on city names.

title: Customer home location column
content: The customers table has a geom column of type geometry(Point,4326) holding each customer's home longitude/latitude, plus a descriptive city column. For 'customers near downtown' or 'customers within X km of a store', use customers.geom::geography with ST_DWithin and distances in metres (multiply km by 1000). Do not filter customers by the city text column for proximity — always use geom.

title: Latitude/longitude columns (no-PostGIS build)
content: When the database does NOT have the PostGIS extension, stores and customers store location as plain latitude and longitude DOUBLE PRECISION columns (degrees, WGS84) instead of a geom column. Detect this with detect_extensions; if PostGIS is absent, compute distance in kilometres with the Haversine formula in plain SQL: 6371 * acos( cos(radians(:lat)) * cos(radians(latitude)) * cos(radians(longitude) - radians(:lon)) + sin(radians(:lat)) * sin(radians(latitude)) ). For 'within X km of downtown' filter that expression <= X using downtown lat 47.6062, lon -122.3321. Return latitude and longitude columns so results can be plotted on the map.

title: Product embedding column (pgvector)
content: The products table has an embedding column of type vector(384) containing a sentence-transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) embedding of the product description. To find products similar in meaning to a phrase, use the semantic_data_search tool on table 'products', embedding_column 'embedding' — do NOT use ILIKE on the description for semantic similarity.

title: Order status values
content: orders.status is one of: 'completed', 'pending', 'cancelled', 'returned'. Revenue and active-customer metrics count ONLY 'completed' orders. 'pending' orders are in-flight and excluded from revenue.

title: Fiscal quarter (definition)
content: The fiscal year starts on February 1. Fiscal Q1 = Feb-Apr, Q2 = May-Jul, Q3 = Aug-Oct, Q4 = Nov-Jan. 'Last quarter' means the most recently completed fiscal quarter relative to the current date, not the calendar quarter.
